text:
My first impression, stepping into the bright, glass-walled hallway, is one of anticipation—a liminal space where entry and exit blur. Everything is still, clinical, and waiting.

Then, the transition: now I am amid vibrant colors, bold words, purposeful displays—a gallery focused on research and development. The narrative on the wall and the interactive screens draw me in, blending intellect with aesthetic curiosity. I feel inquisitive, as though on the threshold of discovery, suspended between the functional and the imaginative. Being here, I experience a quiet thrill: an invitation to reflect, to learn, and to contemplate the bond between innovation and humanity.
